title: Agent Receipt
description: A cryptographically signed record of a single action taken by an AI agent on behalf of a human principal. Validates receipts conforming to the Agent Receipt Protocol v0.1 through v0.5 — the protocol `version` enum on each receipt selects the applicable version; see CHANGELOG.md for what each version adds.

@context.description: JSON-LD context. First two entries MUST be the W3C VC v2 and Agent Receipts context URIs in order. The Agent Receipts context is v1 for receipts at versions 0.1.0–0.4.0 and v2 (which adds issuer.runtime) for 0.5.0+; both remain permanent. Additional context URIs MAY follow.

id.description: Globally unique receipt identifier. MUST be a urn:receipt:<uuid>.

version.description: Protocol version. MUST be one of "0.1.0", "0.2.0", "0.2.1", "0.3.0", "0.4.0", or "0.5.0". Verifiers MUST accept all six.

issuanceDate.description: ISO 8601 datetime when the receipt was issued. MAY differ from action.timestamp in batched scenarios. Uses the VC 1.x field name; see ADR-0009 for the rationale.

allOf.title: Context is pinned to the protocol version
allOf.description: Versions 0.1.0–0.4.0 MUST reference Agent Receipts context v1; 0.5.0 (which adds issuer.runtime) MUST reference v2. This couples the two so a receipt cannot declare a version whose context does not define its fields.

sha256Hash.description: SHA-256 digest in the format sha256:<64 hex chars>.

keyRotation.description: Key-rotation event (ADR-0015). Present only on a key_rotated receipt; a receipt that is not a rotation event MUST NOT include this field. When present, all seven fields are required. The receipt is signed with the OUTGOING key (signed_with: "old"); new_public_key carries the incoming key inline so verifiers can chain through the rotation without an external key registry. See spec §7.3.7.

new_public_key.description: Incoming public key inline: raw key bytes per the algorithm's canonical encoding (Ed25519: the 32-byte public key per RFC 8032 §5.1.5), multibase-encoded with the "u" base64url prefix (the same encoding ADR-0001 defines for proof.proofValue, applied here to raw public-key bytes).

old_key_fingerprint.description: SHA-256 of the outgoing public key's raw bytes (not SPKI/PEM, not a backend handle), as sha256:<lowercase hex>. Index/consistency field — the outgoing key is resolved via proof.verificationMethod or, at genesis, the out-of-band registered key.

new_key_fingerprint.description: SHA-256 of the incoming public key's raw bytes, as sha256:<lowercase hex>. MUST equal the SHA-256 of the bytes decoded from new_public_key.

old_algorithm.description: Algorithm tag of the outgoing key (e.g. "ed25519"). Used to verify the rotation event's own signature, since signed_with is "old".

new_algorithm.description: Algorithm tag of the incoming key (e.g. "ed25519"). Used to verify subsequent receipts. Equal to old_algorithm for same-algorithm rotations.

signed_with.description: Constant "old": the rotation event is signed with the outgoing key, anchoring the transition to the key being retired.

id.description: DID or URI identifying the agent instance.
DID or URI of the entity operating the agent.

runtime.description: Open container for runtime/observability metadata the issuing runtime attaches to the action (ADR-0026). Absent for the root agent. Intentionally NOT additionalProperties:false and typed @json in the JSON-LD context: it carries the members below plus any additional keys a runtime supplies, and new members may be added without a protocol-version bump. Consumers MUST treat unknown keys as untrusted metadata, never as identity.

agent_id.description: Identifier of the sub-agent that issued the receipt. Absent for the root agent.

agent_type.description: Runtime-reported agent type label (e.g. "general-purpose"). Absent for the root agent.

id.description: DID or URI identifying the principal.
Unique action identifier. MUST be act_<uuid>.

type.description: Action type from the taxonomy (§5). Dot-separated hierarchical identifier.

risk_level.description: Risk classification. Implementations MAY escalate but MUST NOT downgrade from taxonomy defaults.

system.description: The system or service acted upon.

resource.description: The specific resource within the system.

parameters_hash.description: SHA-256 hash of the action parameters (RFC 8785 canonical JSON).

oneOf.description: Legacy flat-map form (v0.2.0 / v0.2.1). Operator-controlled stringified field map for human/auditor display.

parameters_disclosure.description: Action parameters disclosure. Either the legacy flat-map (v0.2.0 / v0.2.1) or the HPKE asymmetric encryption envelope defined by ADR-0012 (v0.3.0+). Mode is per-emitter — a receipt MUST NOT mix shapes within this field. `parameters_hash` is the privacy-preserving default and is always authoritative; this field is additive metadata. Operators MUST NOT place plaintext secrets or PII in the legacy flat-map form.

peer_credential.description: OS-attested peer process metadata captured by the daemon at the SDK↔daemon boundary. Present only on receipts emitted through a daemon (ADR-0010); absent on direct SDK emissions. Daemon-attested, not agent-claimed.

pid.description: Peer process ID. POSIX pid_t width (32-bit signed integer).

uid.description: Peer process effective UID. POSIX-only; absent on platforms where UIDs do not apply (e.g. Windows).

gid.description: Peer process effective GID. POSIX-only; absent on platforms where GIDs do not apply (e.g. Windows).

exe_path.description: Best-effort absolute path of the peer process executable. May be absent on systems where the daemon cannot resolve it (locked-down sandboxes, missing /proc, etc.).

emitter_metadata.description: Daemon-observed emitter-side metadata. Currently used for synthetic events_dropped receipts (ADR-0010). Daemon-attested, not agent-claimed.

drop_count.description: Count of audit events the emitter dropped from its in-process buffer before flushing to the daemon.

timestamp.description: ISO 8601 datetime when the action was executed.

trusted_timestamp.description: Base64-encoded RFC 3161 TimeStampToken (DER encoding). Absent when not present; MUST NOT be null.

idempotency_key.description: Optional stable identifier for the logical operation this action represents (e.g. a request ID). When an agent retries a tool call on timeout, the SDK or MCP proxy SHOULD stamp the same idempotency_key on every emitted receipt for that logical operation, so auditors can distinguish a legitimate retry from a duplicated emission. Verifier tooling surfaces duplicate idempotency_key values across a chain as a warning, not a failure — retries are legitimate. Absent when not applicable; MUST NOT be null or empty. See ADR-0019 §S5.

conversation_hash.description: SHA-256 hash of the conversation context.

prompt_preview.description: Plain-text preview of the prompt that triggered the action (may be truncated).

reasoning_hash.description: SHA-256 hash of the agent's reasoning trace.

error.description: Error message if status is "failure". Absent when not applicable; MUST NOT be null.

reversible.description: true if the action can be undone.

reversal_window_seconds.description: Seconds within which reversal is possible.

reversal_of.description: id of the original receipt that this receipt reverses. Present only on reversal receipts.

before_hash.description: SHA-256 hash of relevant state before the action.

after_hash.description: SHA-256 hash of relevant state after the action.

response_hash.description: SHA-256 hash of the RFC 8785 canonical JSON of the server's response, computed after secret redaction (redact → hash → sign). Absent when the issuer did not commit to the response.

scopes.description: Authorization scopes active at time of action.

granted_at.description: ISO 8601 datetime when authorization was granted.

grant_ref.description: Reference to the authorization grant (e.g. a Grantex grant token). Absent when not applicable; MUST NOT be null.

delegation.description: Present when this chain was spawned by delegation from another agent.

parent_chain_id.description: chain_id of the delegating agent's chain.

parent_receipt_id.description: id of the receipt in the parent chain where delegation occurred.

id.description: DID or URI of the delegating agent (the parent chain's issuer).

sequence.description: Monotonically increasing position within the chain. Starts at 1.

previous_receipt_hash.description: SHA-256 hash of the previous receipt's canonical form. MUST be null for the first receipt (sequence: 1).

chain_id.description: Opaque identifier grouping receipts into a logical chain.

terminal.description: When present, asserts this is the final receipt in the chain. MUST be true if present; absence means no claim. Explicit false is not a valid value — the schema restricts this field to the constant true.

status.description: Issuer-asserted reason the chain ended. Only meaningful alongside chain.terminal: true; absence on a terminal receipt is equivalent to "complete". The verifier-derived "unknown" classification (chains with no terminal at all) is never written on the wire — only "complete" and "interrupted" are valid wire values. See spec §7.3.3.

correlation_id.description: Opaque identifier linking related receipts for the same logical tool invocation — e.g. a hook pre-check receipt to the MCP proxy post-action receipt. Populated from the runtime's tool-use correlation token (Claude Code: tool_use_id). Absent when no correlation context is available. MUST be non-empty when present.

created.description: ISO 8601 datetime when the proof was created.

verificationMethod.description: DID URL of the signing key (e.g. "did:agent:...#key-1").

proofValue.description: Multibase-encoded (u-prefixed base64url, no padding) Ed25519 signature over the canonical receipt (proof field excluded).

parametersDisclosureEnvelope.description: Canonical wire shape of credentialSubject.action.parameters_disclosure when the field carries the asymmetric encryption envelope specified by ADR-0012 (v1 envelope). This `$defs` entry is the validator-resolvable form; the sibling spec/schema/parameters-disclosure.schema.json is the documentation-of-record referenced by ADR-0012 and the test-vectors, and MUST stay in sync with this entry.

v.description: Envelope schema version. v1 pins the algorithm choice in `alg`, the single-shot HPKE single-recipient shape, and the field set defined here. Any field addition, removal, encoding change, or AEAD/KEM/KDF change is a v2. The version is a string (not an integer) so that hub re-verifiers can dispatch on the exact JCS-canonical bytes without ambiguity about JSON number encoding.

alg.description: HPKE ciphersuite tag in human-readable form, identifying the KEM / KDF / AEAD triple. v1 pins HPKE base mode with KEM=DHKEM(X25519, HKDF-SHA256) (0x0020), KDF=HKDF-SHA256 (0x0001), AEAD=AES-256-GCM (0x0002) per RFC 9180 §7. A string tag is used rather than the numeric ciphersuite triple so verifiers can dispatch by equality without parsing IANA registry IDs; the numeric triple is recorded in this description as the canonical cross-reference for implementers. Additional ciphersuites require a new ADR-0012 amendment and a v2 envelope.

recipients.description: Recipient descriptors. v1 is single-recipient (minItems=maxItems=1); the array shape is forward-compatible with the Phase C multi-recipient design (see ADR-0012 Phase C). When multi-recipient lands, this constraint relaxes to minItems: 1 with no upper bound and `ct` continues to be a single shared ciphertext under a per-envelope content-encryption key wrapped to each recipient's HPKE encapsulated key. Verifiers MUST treat the array as ordered: the index of a recipient's entry is its position in any future per-recipient wrapped-key array; canonical JSON preserves array order under RFC 8785.

ct.description: AEAD ciphertext including the 16-byte GCM authentication tag, encoded as unpadded base64url (RFC 4648 §5). The `pattern` enforces both the base64url alphabet (no `=` padding, no `+` or `/`) and decodable unpadded-base64url length: complete 4-char groups optionally followed by 2 or 3 trailing chars, which means `len % 4 != 1` — the one length residue that is invalid for base64url without padding. The `minLength` of 24 is the encoded size of the smallest valid v1 AEAD output: v1 plaintext is RFC 8785 canonical JSON of a parameters object (minimum `{}` = 2 bytes), so the minimum ciphertext is 2 + 16 (GCM tag) = 18 bytes → 24 unpadded base64url characters. Plaintext is the RFC 8785 canonical JSON serialisation of the parameters object. The HPKE AAD is the empty string for v1 (single-shot, no out-of-band context binding beyond the receipt envelope itself, which already commits to `parameters_disclosure` by signature). Encoding follows the spec-wide ADR-0009 convention of unpadded base64url for binary fields (see spec §4 `proofValue`); standard base64 is not accepted.

kid.description: Recipient public-key identifier. SHOULD be either an ADR-0007 `did:key` DID URL with a fragment identifying the encryption verification method (e.g. `did:key:z6Mk...#enc-1`) or the `sha256:<hex>` fingerprint convention used elsewhere in the repo (per ADR-0015 "Fingerprint canonical form"). The kid is an index — it lets the forensic responder pick the right private key — and is not by itself authenticated; binding to the recipient public key happens through HPKE encapsulation.

enc.description: HPKE encapsulated key output (RFC 9180 §4.1, `enc`). For the DHKEM(X25519, HKDF-SHA256) KEM this is the 32-byte X25519 ephemeral public key, encoded as unpadded base64url. The `pattern` constrains the value to exactly 43 base64url-alphabet characters — the encoded length of 32 raw bytes with no padding (`ceil(32 * 4 / 3)` rounded up, minus 1 padding character that would normally complete the final 4-char block). The HPKE specification names this field `enc`; this schema uses the same name rather than an invented alternative (e.g. `wrapped_key`, `epk`, `encap`) so implementers can map directly to library APIs without translation. The original ADR-0012 sketch listed `encap` — that name is superseded here; see the ADR amendment for the rationale.
